items.title: Kumanjayi White's custody death investigation moves incrementally one year on

children.content: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

gallery.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: Population of NT town doubles overnight as flood evacuees arrive

children.content: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

image.altText: An aerial image of a small town.

items.title: 'Sleep tight baby': Jurors in NT murder trial to see 'confronting' footage

children.content: Jurors in the trial for the alleged 2024 murder of Yiel Gatluak in Alice Springs have heard they will see "a degree of violence most of you probably won't be accustomed to".

image.altText: Close up of a photo of a young Sudanese man, in a suit and tie, white shirt, phone case, hands holding it.

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave NT tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

gallery.altText: A reception area full of mud, with flood debris over the counter.

thumbnail.altText: A reception area full of mud, with flood debris over the counter.

avatar.alt: A journalist holding a notebook and microphone, speaking to the camera.

items.title: Vision loss and self-harm — government warned of custody health risks

children.content: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

image.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: NT fuel retailers forced to reveal full costs as 77yo law revived

children.content: The Northern Territory government has triggered a decades old law in an attempt to combat fuel price gouging at the bowser.

gallery.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

thumbnail.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

items.title: Artists left devastated after floods destroy renowned NT arts centre

children.content: Artists from the remote Northern Territory community of Nauiyu are facing a devastating loss after the Merrepen Arts Centre was destroyed by recent flooding.

image.altText: Kieren Karritpul, an Indigenous man wearing a pattern shirt, stands in front of several paintings hanging on the wall.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 19yo charged with murder after fatal stabbing in Katherine 'fist fight'

children.content: Police have charged a 19-year-old man with murder after he allegedly stabbed two other teenagers in Katherine on Sunday, leaving one dead and the other with critical injuries.

image.altText: Two police vehicles on street road, crime scene tape in front of cars, dark night, flashing lights on one car.

items.title: The ABC rode along with paramedics to examine the NT service's under-resourcing

children.content: Warren Purse, a Territory paramedic with over 20 years' experience, is assaulted on the job about three or four times a year.

image.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

articles.title: St John NT warns 'extraordinary' weekend measures 'absolutely not' safe

children.content: Northern Territory paramedics pushed "well and truly beyond our limits" at the weekend say the "extraordinary" measures taken in response are unsustainable and "absolutely not" safe.

image.altText: Female paramedic looks ahead with serious face

articles.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

items.title: PM announces gambling ad ban in stadiums, during school pick-up

children.content: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: States and territories agree to extra fuel excise cut using GST revenue

children.content: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

image.altText: A person fuels up their vehicle at a petrol station close up.

items.title: Sandra's brain is failing her and she lives in 'extreme pain' from her vaccination injury

children.content: For years these vaccine-injured Australians have been trying to get compensation from a 'torturous' government compensation scheme.

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: New pact affirms NZ as Pacific nation's partner of choice after spat over China

children.content: New Zealand and the Cook Islands have mended their relationship with a pact that restores funding to the Pacific island nation.

image.altText: Two ships with blue hulls and white bridges in a harbour with forested mountains in the background.

items.title: In pictures: Artemis II NASA's first mission to the Moon in decades

image.altText: The NASA Artemis II SLS rocket launching.

children.content: For the first time in more than 50 years NASA has sent astronauts around the Moon, marking a major milestone in a new era of space exploration.

items.title: Sit back and let us take you to the Moon with Artemis II

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

items.title: Kerr in, but several Matildas stars not part of squad for FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr has been named, but the Matildas will be missing a number of their other big guns when they travel to Kenya for the team's maiden FIFA Series in April.

image.altText: Mary Fowler of Australia during the AFC Women’s Asian Cup dribbling the ball during a match

items.title: Activist ship deliberately collides with trawler as 'krill wars' escalate

children.content: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

image.altText: View from the deck of a ship as it crashes into another ship.

items.title: Here is the key to getting through The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

children.content: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

items.title: When can I start taking my kids running with me?

children.content: If you enjoy running and your child has shown interest in coming along, you might be wondering when they're old enough to start. Experts say there are a few ways you can determine if they're ready.

image.altText: Man and child running together

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia

children.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

image.altText: A close up of a plastic dinosaur.

items.title: Thousands lost to cancellations as fuel crisis bites tourism sector

children.content: Tourism businesses say they're bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

items.title: Class action filed against Kmart alleges 'systemic' wage underpayment

children.content: Kmart managers have alleged they were routinely required to work "well beyond" their rostered hours without receiving overtime rates, penalty rates, or prescribed allowances.

items.title: Bondi gunman fails to have family's identities suppressed for 40 years

children.content: Lawyers for Bondi gunman Naveed Akram fail in a bid to have the identities of his mother, brother and sister protected by a decades-long court suppression order on safety grounds.

items.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued

children.content: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

items.title: What Australia can learn from French cities that made public transport free

children.content: There are growing calls in Australia for free public transport amid the Iran war with two states putting in place free services on a temporary basis. But in France, almost 50 cities have free public transport.

children.content: Jessica tried to keep her distance from Harrods billionaire owner Mohamed Al Fayed, who was known to abuse his female staff, until he spotted her going down an escalator.

items.title: Weather to deliver Easter treat, but another cyclone looms next week
